title: Pandanus pseudolinnaei
taxonomy: Pandanus pseudolinnaei is classified as a species within the genus Pandanus. The genus Pandanus is part of the family Pandanaceae, which is a family of monocot flowering plants. Pandanaceae is a family within the order Pandanales. The order Pandanales is a part of the clade Arecidae, which includes various palm-like and related plants. Pandanus pseudolinnaei is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia.

parts: Pandanus pseudolinnaei is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Africa, specifically in the countries of Malawi, Mozambique, and Tanzania. The medicinal parts of Pandanus pseudolinnaei are the leaves and fruit, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The leaves of the plant are rich in antioxidants and are used to treat fever, while the fruit is used to treat diarrhea and other gastrointestinal problems.
